---
title: "Project 1(Plastic)"
author: "Tyler Kinney, Greyson Ballesteros"
format: 
  html:
    code-tools: true
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: journal
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
editor: source
execute:
  echo: true
  error: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: packages-setup
library(tidyverse)
library(glue)
library(here)
library(jsonlite)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggridges)
library(gt)
library(scales)
library(ggtext)
```

### Research Question 1

RQ: Has the types of plastics that are being used changed significantly from 2019 to 2020?

## Data Context

```{r}
#| label: load_data
tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load('2021-01-26')
plastics <- tuesdata$plastics
```

## Summary Tables

```{r}
#| label: Summary 1 (non-modified, iteration, custom function)

plastic_types <- c("hdpe", "ldpe", "pet", "pp", "ps", "pvc", "o")

Prop_change <- function(type) {
  plastics |>
    filter(parent_company != "Grand Total") |>
    group_by(year) |>
    summarize(
      total_type = sum(across(type), na.rm = TRUE),
      total_all = sum(grand_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    mutate(prop = total_type / total_all) |>
    arrange(year) |>
    summarize(
      plastic_type = type,
      prop_2019 = prop[year == 2019],
      prop_2020 = prop[year == 2020],
      change = prop_2020 - prop_2019
    )
}

map_dfr(plastic_types, Prop_change) |>
  arrange(change) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = md("**Proportions of Each Plastic Type**"),
             subtitle = "and Changes in Those proportions (2020-2019)") |>
  fmt_percent(columns = c(`change`, prop_2019, prop_2020),
              decimals = 2) |>
  cols_label(plastic_type = "Type",
             `prop_2019` = "2019",
             `prop_2020` = "2020",
             change = "Change") |>
  tab_options(heading.background.color = "#EB6864",
              column_labels.background.color = "lightgrey",
              row.striping.background_color = "lightgrey",
              row.striping.include_table_body = TRUE) |>
  tab_style(style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
            locations = cells_column_labels(everything())) |>
  tab_style(style = cell_borders(sides = "bottom",
            color = "#333333",
            weight = px(2)),
            locations = list(cells_column_labels(), cells_title(groups = "subtitle"))) |>
  data_color(columns = change,
             colors = col_numeric(
             palette = c("lightgreen", "white", "#D95C5C"),
             domain = NULL))
  
```


```{r}
#| label: Summary 2 (non-modified)

plastics |>
  filter(parent_company != "Grand Total") |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(hdpe, ldpe, o, pet, pp, ps, pvc, grand_total),
               names_to = "plastic_type",
               values_to = "amount") |>
  group_by(plastic_type, year) |>
  summarize(total = sum(amount, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(plastic_type, year) |>
  group_by(plastic_type) |>
  mutate(`2019_2020_YoY` = (total - lag(total)) / lag(total)) |>
  filter(!is.na(`2019_2020_YoY`)) |>
  select(-total, -year) |>
  arrange(`2019_2020_YoY`) |>
  ungroup() |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = md("**Year Over Year Increase in Types of Plastic Use**"),
             subtitle = "Calculated from 2020") |>
  fmt_percent(columns = `2019_2020_YoY`,
              decimals = 2) |>
  cols_label(plastic_type = "Type",
             `2019_2020_YoY` = "YoY Increase") |>
  tab_options(heading.background.color = "#EB6864",
              column_labels.background.color = "lightgrey",
              row.striping.background_color = "lightgrey",
              row.striping.include_table_body = TRUE) |>
  tab_style(style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
            locations = cells_column_labels(everything())) |>
  tab_style(style = cell_borders(sides = "bottom",
            color = "#333333",
            weight = px(2)),
            locations = list(cells_column_labels(), cells_title(groups = "subtitle"))) |>
  data_color(columns = `2019_2020_YoY`,
             colors = col_numeric(
             palette = c("lightgreen", "white", "#D95C5C"),
             domain = NULL))
  

```


## Data Visualizations

```{r}
#| label: Visual 1

plastics |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(hdpe, ldpe, o, pet, pp, ps, pvc),
               names_to = "plastic_type",
               values_to = "plastic_amount") |>
  filter(parent_company != "Grand Total",
         !is.na(plastic_amount)) |>
  group_by(year, plastic_type) |>
  summarize(total = sum(plastic_amount, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year), y = total, fill = plastic_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent()) +
  labs(title = "Proportion of Plastic Types by Year",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "Plastic Type") +
  theme_light() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")
```

```{r}
#| label: Visual 2

plastics |>
  pivot_longer(cols = hdpe:pvc,
               names_to = "type",
               values_to = "count") |>
  filter(parent_company != "Grand Total",
         !is.na(count)) |>
  group_by(year, type) |>
  summarize(total = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(year) |>
  mutate(prop = total / sum(total)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(year, type, prop) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = year,
              values_from = prop,
              names_prefix = "year_") |>
  mutate(change = year_2020 - year_2019) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_segment(aes(x = year_2019, xend = year_2020,
               y = type, yend = type),
               color = "black",
               linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(x = year_2019, y = type), color = "#4C78A8", size = 3) +
  geom_point(aes(x = year_2020, y = type), color = "#E45756", size = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent()) +
  labs(title = "Change in Plastic Type Composition",,
       subtitle = "(<span style='color:#4C78A8;'>2019</span> – <span style='color:#E45756;'>2020</span>)",
       x = "Proportion of total plastic",
       y = "Plastic type") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(plot.subtitle = element_markdown())

```

### Research Question 2


## Data Context


## Summary Tables


## Data Visualizations
